title: Fitness Influencers Linked to Illegal Steroid Market
subtitle: New investigation reveals how wellness brands and social media stars are secretly selling dangerous performance-enhancing drugs.

summary.en: A recent investigation has uncovered a connection between fitness influencers and an illegal online steroid market. These influencers, who represent a wellness brand, are using Telegram to sell drugs and recruit new members. Anabolic steroids are synthetic hormones that can cause serious health problems, including heart and liver damage. While some people use these substances to change their bodies quickly, experts warn that the risks are often ignored. The story highlights the growing pressure on social media to achieve a perfect body image.
summary.zh: 近期的一項調查揭露了健身網紅與非法線上類固醇市場之間的關聯。這些為健康保健品牌代言的網紅，正利用 Telegram 販售藥物並招募新成員。合成代謝類固醇是人工合成的荷爾蒙，可能導致心臟與肝臟受損等嚴重健康問題。儘管有些人使用這些物質來快速改變體態，但專家警告，其風險往往被忽視。這則報導凸顯了社群媒體上追求完美體態的壓力日益增加。

paragraphs.en: A new investigation by The Guardian has revealed a hidden link between fitness influencers and an illegal market for performance-enhancing drugs. These influencers, who publicly represent a wellness company called Gencore Global, are reportedly using an underground network on the messaging app Telegram to sell steroids and other substances. Gencore Global presents itself as a legitimate health and wellness business in the UK, selling products like skincare and supplements. However, the report found that several people linked to the brand are actively promoting illegal drugs to their followers.
paragraphs.zh: 《衛報》（The Guardian）的一項最新調查揭露了健身網紅與非法增強體能藥物市場之間的隱密連結。這些網紅公開為一家名為「健核全球」（Gencore Global）的健康保健公司代言，據報導，他們正利用通訊軟體 Telegram 上的地下網絡販售類固醇及其他藥物。健核全球對外宣稱是一家位於英國的合法健康保健企業，販售護膚品與營養補充品等產品。然而，調查發現，多名與該品牌有關的人士正積極向其追隨者推銷非法藥物。

paragraphs.en: Anabolic steroids are man-made versions of the male hormone testosterone. They are often used to help build muscle and improve recovery after exercise. While it is legal to own these drugs for personal use in the UK, it is against the law to sell or supply them without a special license. The Telegram network, which has operated under names like Rohm and Anabolic By Nature, offers a variety of products. These include steroids, growth hormones, and experimental substances known as peptides. Peptides are small chains of proteins that occur naturally in the body, but some versions are being sold for weight loss or anti-aging without proper scientific proof that they are safe for humans.
paragraphs.zh: 合成代謝類固醇是人工合成的男性荷爾蒙睪固酮。它們常被用於增強肌肉並改善運動後的恢復。雖然在英國持有這些藥物供個人使用是合法的，但未經特殊許可進行販售或供應則屬違法。這個在 Telegram 上運作的網絡曾以「Rohm」和「Anabolic By Nature」等名稱經營，提供多種產品，包括類固醇、生長激素以及被稱為「胜肽」的實驗性物質。胜肽是人體內自然存在的蛋白質短鏈，但有些版本在缺乏足夠科學證據證明對人體安全的情況下，被當作減重或抗衰老產品販售。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the influencers involved have a combined following of over 180,000 people on platforms like TikTok and Instagram. They use these accounts to recruit people into the secret Telegram chats, where they offer advice and sell products. One person acting as an administrator in the group even joked about the illegal nature of their business, saying it was unrealistic to expect an underground shop to be open all the time. Experts are concerned that the line between healthy fitness culture and the promotion of dangerous substances is becoming harder to see.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》報導，涉案網紅在 TikTok 和 Instagram 等平台上的追隨者總數超過 18 萬人。他們利用這些帳號將民眾招募進私密的 Telegram 群組，在群組內提供建議並販售產品。其中一名擔任群組管理員的人甚至拿非法經營一事開玩笑，稱要求地下商店全天候營業是不切實際的。專家擔憂，健康健身文化與推廣危險藥物之間的界線正變得越來越模糊。

paragraphs.en: Using these drugs without medical supervision carries significant health risks. Doctors warn that users may face problems such as high blood pressure, heart disease, liver damage, and mood changes like aggression. Jamie Mantzouridis, a 29-year-old former bodybuilder, shared his own experience with the dangers of these substances. He started using steroids in his early 20s because he felt pressure to look like the larger men he saw at the gym. He explained that while steroids can help someone get results quickly, they are not a "cheat code" and come with serious consequences.
paragraphs.zh: 在沒有醫療監督的情況下使用這些藥物會帶來嚴重的健康風險。醫師警告，使用者可能面臨高血壓、心臟病、肝臟受損以及情緒改變（如攻擊性增強）等問題。29 歲的前健美選手傑米·曼祖里迪斯（Jamie Mantzouridis）分享了他親身經歷這些藥物危害的過程。他在 20 多歲時開始使用類固醇，因為他感受到必須練得像健身房裡那些壯碩男性一樣的壓力。他解釋說，雖然類固醇能讓人快速看到成果，但這並非「作弊代碼」，且伴隨著嚴重的後果。

paragraphs.en: During his time using these drugs, Mantzouridis suffered from severe acne and an infection that required a stay in the hospital. He also experimented with insulin, a drug typically used by people with diabetes, which can be life-threatening if used incorrectly by a healthy person. He eventually decided to stop after a frightening experience where he felt dizzy and close to collapsing. Today, Mantzouridis works as a nutritionist and personal trainer. He believes that social media creates unrealistic expectations for men, making them feel that they must look a certain way to be successful.
paragraphs.zh: 在使用這些藥物的期間，曼祖里迪斯飽受嚴重痤瘡之苦，還因感染而住院。他甚至嘗試使用胰島素，這通常是糖尿病患者使用的藥物，若健康人士使用不當，可能危及生命。在經歷了一次感到頭暈且瀕臨昏倒的驚險事件後，他最終決定停用。如今，曼祖里迪斯擔任營養師與私人教練。他認為社群媒體為男性創造了不切實際的期待，讓他們覺得必須擁有特定的外貌才能獲得成功。

paragraphs.en: This situation highlights how easily young people can be influenced by fitness stars online. The pressure to achieve a perfect body can lead individuals to seek shortcuts, often through dangerous and illegal channels. As the investigation shows, even brands that appear professional and legitimate may be hiding darker activities behind the scenes. For those looking to improve their health, experts continue to emphasize that there is no substitute for safe, natural training and proper nutrition. The risks associated with these underground markets are not just legal, but deeply personal and potentially life-changing.
paragraphs.zh: 此情況凸顯了年輕人有多容易受到線上健身明星的影響。追求完美體態的壓力可能導致個人尋求捷徑，而這些捷徑往往透過危險且非法的管道。正如調查所顯示，即使是看起來專業且合法的品牌，幕後也可能隱藏著更黑暗的勾當。對於那些尋求改善健康的人，專家持續強調，安全、自然的訓練與正確的營養攝取是無可取代的。這些地下市場帶來的風險不僅涉及法律問題，更會對個人造成深遠且可能改變一生的影響。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A boundary or distinction between two different things or ideas.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指健康文化與危險物質推廣之間的界線，而非實體的線。文中：Experts are concerned that the line between healthy fitness culture and the promotion of dangerous substances is becoming harder to see.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A quick or unfair way to achieve a result that usually requires hard work.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 作弊碼（比喻走捷徑、不勞而獲的方法）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 源自電玩遊戲術語，這裡比喻類固醇並非獲得肌肉的捷徑。文中：He explained that while steroids can help someone get results quickly, they are not a "cheat code" and come with serious consequences.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Happening secretly or out of public view.
keyTerms.noteZh: 形容隱藏在公開形象之下的非法活動。文中：As the investigation shows, even brands that appear professional and legitimate may be hiding darker activities behind the scenes.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Fitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on Telegram

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: ‘I felt dizzy’: bodybuilder recalls how drug abuse caught up with him

quiz.question: What does the article state about the legality of anabolic steroids in the UK?

options.A: They are completely illegal to possess under any circumstances.
options.B: It is lawful to possess them for personal use, but illegal to sell them without a license.
options.C: They are legal to sell as long as they are marketed as health supplements.
options.D: They are only legal if prescribed by a doctor for diabetes treatment.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，文中明確指出在英國持有類固醇供個人使用是合法的，但未經許可販售或供應則是違法的。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章並未禁止個人持有；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章提到非法販售並未提及任何合法販售途徑；選項 D 則混淆了類固醇與文中提到的胰島素（insulin），胰島素是用於糖尿病治療，而非類固醇。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might Gencore Global be considered a deceptive company?

options.A: It openly encourages its followers to purchase illegal substances on social media.
options.B: It sells skincare and supplements that have been proven to be harmful to users.
options.C: It maintains a professional public image while its associates secretly facilitate illegal drug sales.
options.D: It forces its influencers to use experimental peptides to improve their physical appearance.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第一段提到 Gencore Global 將自己呈現為合法的健康與保健企業，販售護膚品與補充劑，但調查卻發現其相關人員在幕後利用 Telegram 販售非法藥物。選項 A 錯誤，因為公司表面上是合法的；選項 B 錯誤，文章未提及該公司的護膚品有害；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未指出公司「強迫」網紅使用藥物，而是網紅主動參與非法活動。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the author mean when describing steroids as not a 'cheat code'?

options.A: They do not provide an easy or guaranteed shortcut to success without negative consequences.
options.B: They are not actually effective at building muscle mass or improving recovery.
options.C: They are too expensive for the average person to purchase regularly.
options.D: They are not recognized by professional sports organizations as performance enhancers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。作者在文中引用 Jamie Mantzouridis 的話，指出類固醇雖然能快速見效，但並非「作弊碼（cheat code）」，因為它伴隨著嚴重的健康代價。這意味著它不是一個沒有副作用的捷徑。選項 B 錯誤，因為文章承認類固醇確實能幫助增肌；選項 C 與 D 則偏離了文章對於「作弊碼」一詞在健康風險脈絡下的隱喻意義。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The UK government should legalize the sale of all performance-enhancing drugs to ensure safety.
options.B: Fitness influencers are the most reliable sources of information for health and nutrition.
options.C: Social media platforms should be shut down to prevent the spread of illegal drug markets.
options.D: The pursuit of a 'perfect' body via online influencers can lead to dangerous and illegal health risks.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。本文的核心主旨在於探討健身網紅如何透過社群媒體影響年輕人，導致他們為了追求理想體態而接觸危險且非法的藥物市場。文章透過調查報告與前健美選手的經驗分享，強調了這種行為的嚴重後果。選項 A 與 C 為過激的政策建議，文中並未主張；選項 B 與文章論點完全相反，文章正是要提醒讀者不要盲目相信網紅。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
